summary: First-finish smoke test of SSK Footcare ERP. Auth, RBAC, materials, styles, costing preview, POs, production jobs, defects, dashboard, and AI PO extraction all functional. Frontend login + 8 sidebar pages load without crash. Production Kanban renders all 9 stages. ONE backend bug found: POST /api/users returns 500 due to ObjectId serialization (user is still created in DB, but response fails — blocks admin user-creation UX).
passed: GET /api/materials with auth -> 200 (11 seeded items); without auth -> 401
GET /api/dashboard/stats -> 200, stage_counts has all 9 keys (procurement, cutting, folding, attachment, stitching, lasting, sole_pasting, finishing, dispatched), revenue=491400, pairs_in_wip=2880
GET /api/production/jobs -> 200, returns 20 jobs from seeded PO with history array
POST /api/pos/extract with Siyaram PDF -> 200, po_number=2220010098, client=SIYARAM SILK MILLS LTD., 20 line_items, grand_total=491400.0 (Gemini extraction working)
Frontend login flow works (Sign In button)
All 8 sidebar pages (Dashboard, Styles, Materials, Costing, Purchase Orders, Production, Defects & QC, Users) load without crash
Production.jsx STAGES array (lines 6-16) contains all 9 expected stage keys: procurement, cutting, folding, attachment, stitching, lasting, sole_pasting, finishing, dispatched
Production Kanban UI renders columns (Procurement, Cutting, Folding, Attachment visible; horizontal scroll for rest)
Backend correctly creates new production jobs at 'procurement' stage (server.py line 407)

failed.issue: POST /api/users returns 500 Internal Server Error. User row IS inserted (subsequent login succeeds), but FastAPI cannot serialize the response: TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable. Likely returning raw Mongo doc with _id ObjectId instead of mapping to model with str id.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/users -> 'Internal Server Error' HTTP 500. Backend traceback: 'fastapi/encoders.py ... ObjectId object is not iterable ... vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute'. Reproducible with any new email payload.

failed.issue: Existing production_jobs from earlier seed contain legacy stage value 'fitting' in their history array, which is not part of the current 9-stage enum. New PATCHes correctly use new stages. Not a blocker but main agent may want to clean DB or filter history display.

action_items: Fix POST /api/users response serialization in /app/backend/server.py — return a UserOut model (with id as str) instead of the raw Mongo insert result / dict containing ObjectId. Also wrap in try/except for duplicate email -> 409.
Optional: backfill/migrate any production_jobs with stage='fitting' to a valid stage (e.g. 'cutting' or 'folding'), or ignore in dashboard.
